paper_metadata.title: A Generalist Agent
paper_metadata.abstract: Inspired by progress in large-scale language modeling, we apply a similar approach towards building a single generalist agent beyond the realm of text outputs. The agent, which we refer to as Gato, works as a multi-modal, multi-task, multi-embodiment generalist policy. The same network with the same weights can play Atari, caption images, chat, stack blocks with a real robot arm and much more, deciding based on its context whether to output text, joint torques, button presses, or other tokens. In this report we describe the model and the data, and document the current capabilities of Gato.

extraction_data.innovation: A single transformer sequence model that can act as a multi‑modal, multi‑task, multi‑embodiment generalist policy by serializing diverse data into a flat token sequence.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: MassiveText, ALIGN (1.8B image‑text pairs), LTIP (312M image‑text pairs), Conceptual Captions (3.3M), COCO Captions (120k), MultiModal MassiveWeb (43M webpages), OKVQA (9k), VQAv2 (443k)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Fine‑tuning on task‑specific data such as robotic stacking demonstrations
extraction_data.extension: Modality‑specific embedding function that processes tokens differently depending on whether they originate from text, discrete, or continuous observations/actions
extraction_data.research_problem: Building a generalist agent capable of performing a wide variety of tasks across different modalities and embodiments with a single set of weights
extraction_data.application: Simulated control tasks, Atari games, BabyAI, Meta‑World, DM Control Suite, robotic block‑stacking (both simulation and real), vision‑language tasks, and other multi‑modal benchmarks.
